heading: Coverdell education savings accounts
label: 530 Coverdell education savings accounts
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (d)(4)(B)(iv), is the date of enactment of , which enacted this section and was approved .Pub. L. 105–34Aug. 5, 1997

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: A Coverdell education savings account shall be exempt from taxation under this subtitle. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, the Coverdell education savings account shall be subject to the taxes imposed by section 511 (relating to imposition of tax on unrelated business income of charitable organizations).

children.label: i
children.text: unless it is in cash,

children.label: ii
children.text: after the date on which such beneficiary attains age 18, or

children.label: iii
children.text: except in the case of rollover contributions, if such contribution would result in aggregate contributions for the taxable year exceeding $2,000.

children.label: B
children.text: The trustee is a bank (as defined in section 408(n)) or another person who demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the manner in which that person will administer the trust will be consistent with the requirements of this section or who has so demonstrated with respect to any individual retirement plan.

children.label: C
children.text: No part of the trust assets will be invested in life insurance contracts.

children.label: D
children.text: The assets of the trust shall not be commingled with other property except in a common trust fund or common investment fund.

children.label: E
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (d)(7), any balance to the credit of the designated beneficiary on the date on which the beneficiary attains age 30 shall be distributed within 30 days after such date to the beneficiary or, if the beneficiary dies before attaining age 30, shall be distributed within 30 days after the date of death of such beneficiary.

children.label: i
children.text: qualified higher education expenses (as defined in section 529(e)(3)), and

children.label: ii
children.text: qualified elementary and secondary education expenses (as defined in paragraph (3)).

children.heading: Qualified tuition programs
children.label: B Qualified tuition programs
children.text: Such term shall include any contribution to a qualified tuition program (as defined in section 529(b)) on behalf of the designated beneficiary (as defined in section 529(e)(1)); but there shall be no increase in the investment in the contract for purposes of applying section 72 by reason of any portion of such contribution which is not includible in gross income by reason of subsection (d)(2).

children.heading: Qualified elementary and secondary education expenses
children.label: 3 Qualified elementary and secondary education expenses
i
children.text: expenses for tuition, fees, academic tutoring, special needs services in the case of a special needs beneficiary, books, supplies, and other equipment which are incurred in connection with the enrollment or attendance of the designated beneficiary of the trust as an elementary or secondary school student at a public, private, or religious school,

children.label: ii
children.text: expenses for room and board, uniforms, transportation, and supplementary items and services (including extended day programs) which are required or provided by a public, private, or religious school in connection with such enrollment or attendance, and

children.label: iii
children.text: expenses for the purchase of any computer technology or equipment or Internet access and related services, if such technology, equipment, or services are to be used by the beneficiary and the beneficiary’s family during any of the years the beneficiary is in school.

children.heading: School
children.label: B School
children.text: The term “school” means any school which provides elementary education or secondary education (kindergarten through grade 12), as determined under State law.

children.heading: Computer technology or equipment
children.label: C Computer technology or equipment
children.text: The term “computer technology or equipment” means computer software (as defined by section 197(e)(3)(B)), computer or peripheral equipment (as defined by section 168(i)(2)(B)), and fiber optic cable related to computer use.

children.heading: Time when contributions deemed made
children.label: 4 Time when contributions deemed made
children.text: An individual shall be deemed to have made a contribution to an education individual retirement account on the last day of the preceding taxable year if the contribution is made on account of such taxable year and is made not later than the time prescribed by law for filing the return for such taxable year (not including extensions thereof).

children.heading: Reduction in permitted contributions based on adjusted gross income
children.label: c Reduction in permitted contributions based on adjusted gross income
i
children.text: the contributor’s modified adjusted gross income for such taxable year, over

children.label: ii
children.text: $95,000 ($190,000 in the case of a joint return), bears to

children.label: B
children.text: $15,000 ($30,000 in the case of a joint return).

children.heading: Modified adjusted gross income
children.label: 2 Modified adjusted gross income
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “modified adjusted gross income” means the adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year increased by any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any distribution shall be includible in the gross income of the distributee in the manner as provided in section 72.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No amount shall be includible in gross income under paragraph (1) if the qualified education expenses of the designated beneficiary during the taxable year are not less than the aggregate distributions during the taxable year.

children.heading: Distributions in excess of expenses
children.label: B Distributions in excess of expenses
children.text: If such aggregate distributions exceed such expenses during the taxable year, the amount otherwise includible in gross income under paragraph (1) shall be reduced by the amount which bears the same ratio to the amount which would be includible in gross income under paragraph (1) (without regard to this subparagraph) as the qualified education expenses bear to such aggregate distributions.

children.heading: Coordination with American Opportunity and Lifetime Learning credits and qualified tuition programs
children.label: C Coordination with American Opportunity and Lifetime Learning credits and qualified tuition programs
I
children.text: as provided in section 25A(g)(2), and

children.label: II
children.text: by the amount of such expenses which were taken into account in determining the credit allowed to the taxpayer or any other person under section 25A.

children.heading: Coordination with qualified tuition programs
children.label: ii Coordination with qualified tuition programs
I
children.text: the aggregate distributions during such year to which subparagraph (A) and section 529(c)(3)(B) apply, exceed

children.label: II
children.text: the total amount of qualified education expenses (after the application of clause (i)) for such year,

children.heading: Disallowance of excluded amounts as deduction, credit, or exclusion
children.label: D Disallowance of excluded amounts as deduction, credit, or exclusion
children.text: No deduction, credit, or exclusion shall be allowed to the taxpayer under any other section of this chapter for any qualified education expenses to the extent taken into account in determining the amount of the exclusion under this paragraph.

children.heading: Special rules for applying estate and gift taxes with respect to account
children.label: 3 Special rules for applying estate and gift taxes with respect to account
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of paragraphs (2), (4), and (5) of section 529(c) shall apply for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Additional tax for distributions not used for educational expenses
children.label: 4 Additional tax for distributions not used for educational expenses

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The tax imposed by this chapter for any taxable year on any taxpayer who receives a payment or distribution from a Coverdell education savings account which is includible in gross income shall be increased by 10 percent of the amount which is so includible.

children.label: i
children.text: made to a beneficiary (or to the estate of the designated beneficiary) on or after the death of the designated beneficiary,

children.label: ii
children.text: attributable to the designated beneficiary’s being disabled (within the meaning of section 72(m)(7)),

children.label: iii
children.text: made on account of a scholarship, allowance, or payment described in section 25A(g)(2) received by the designated beneficiary to the extent the amount of the payment or distribution does not exceed the amount of the scholarship, allowance, or payment,

children.label: iv
children.text: made on account of the attendance of the designated beneficiary at the United States Military Academy, the United States Naval Academy, the United States Air Force Academy, the United States Coast Guard Academy, or the United States Merchant Marine Academy, to the extent that the amount of the payment or distribution does not exceed the costs of advanced education (as defined by , United States Code, as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section) attributable to such attendance, orsection 2005(e)(3) of title 10

children.label: v
children.text: an amount which is includible in gross income solely by application of paragraph (2)(C)(i)(II) for the taxable year.

children.heading: Contributions returned before certain date
children.label: C Contributions returned before certain date
i
children.text: such distribution is made before the first day of the sixth month of the taxable year following the taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such distribution is accompanied by the amount of net income attributable to such excess contribution.

children.heading: Rollover contributions
children.label: 5 Rollover contributions
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any amount paid or distributed from a Coverdell education savings account to the extent that the amount received is paid, not later than the 60th day after the date of such payment or distribution, into another Coverdell education savings account for the benefit of the same beneficiary or a member of the family (within the meaning of section 529(e)(2)) of such beneficiary who has not attained age 30 as of such date. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any payment or distribution if it applied to any prior payment or distribution during the 12-month period ending on the date of the payment or distribution.

children.heading: Change in beneficiary
children.label: 6 Change in beneficiary
children.text: Any change in the beneficiary of a Coverdell education savings account shall not be treated as a distribution for purposes of paragraph (1) if the new beneficiary is a member of the family (as so defined) of the old beneficiary and has not attained age 30 as of the date of such change.

children.heading: Special rules for death and divorce
children.label: 7 Special rules for death and divorce
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of paragraphs (7) and (8) of section 220(f) shall apply. In applying the preceding sentence, members of the family (as so defined) of the designated beneficiary shall be treated in the same manner as the spouse under such paragraph (8).

children.heading: Deemed distribution on required distribution date
children.label: 8 Deemed distribution on required distribution date
children.text: In any case in which a distribution is required under subsection (b)(1)(E), any balance to the credit of a designated beneficiary as of the close of the 30-day period referred to in such subsection for making such distribution shall be deemed distributed at the close of such period.

children.label: i
children.text: the sum of the amounts received during such period by such contributor under such sections with respect to such person, reduced by

children.label: ii
children.text: the amounts so received which were contributed to a Roth IRA under section 408A(e)(2) or to another Coverdell education savings account.

children.heading: Annual limit on number of rollovers not to apply
children.label: B Annual limit on number of rollovers not to apply
children.text: The last sentence of paragraph (5) shall not apply with respect to amounts treated as a rollover by subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Application of section 72
children.label: C Application of section 72
children.text: For purposes of applying section 72 in the case of a distribution which is includible in gross income under paragraph (1), the amount treated as a rollover by reason of subparagraph (A) shall be treated as investment in the contract.

children.heading: Tax treatment of accounts
children.label: e Tax treatment of accounts
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of paragraphs (2) and (4) of section 408(e) shall apply to any Coverdell education savings account.

children.heading: Community property laws
children.label: f Community property laws
children.text: This section shall be applied without regard to any community property laws.

children.heading: Custodial accounts
children.label: g Custodial accounts
children.text: For purposes of this section, a custodial account shall be treated as a trust if the assets of such account are held by a bank (as defined in section 408(n)) or another person who demonstrates, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, that the manner in which he will administer the account will be consistent with the requirements of this section, and if the custodial account would, except for the fact that it is not a trust, constitute an account described in subsection (b)(1). For purposes of this title, in the case of a custodial account treated as a trust by reason of the preceding sentence, the custodian of such account shall be treated as the trustee thereof.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: h Reports
children.text: The trustee of a Coverdell education savings account shall make such reports regarding such account to the Secretary and to the beneficiary of the account with respect to contributions, distributions, and such other matters as the Secretary may require. The reports required by this subsection shall be filed at such time and in such manner and furnished to such individuals at such time and in such manner as may be required.
